orphaned_subagents.reason: detect-orphan-agents.sh reported not referenced by any command. HOWEVER: codex adversarial review identified this as a false positive — product-owner IS referenced in commands/dev.md, commands/dev-overnight.md, agents/ba.md, agents/pm.md as a specialist agent type. The detection script performs a simple text match and misses structural references. This finding is advisory only.
orphaned_subagents.note: false_positive_likely — verified by codex: product-owner agent is referenced dynamically as a specialist type in dev.md, dev-overnight.md, ba.md, pm.md

unreferenced_scripts.reason: No static references in commands/agents/scripts. Script contains installation setup for /usr/local/sbin/tmp-cleanup.sh (operational cron cleanup). One-time install script with 1 commit, 7 days old.
unreferenced_scripts.recommendation: needs_user_confirmation — operational install script; verify whether /usr/local/sbin/tmp-cleanup.sh was already installed from this source before considering deletion

unreferenced_scripts.reason: No static references in commands/agents/scripts. One-time helper, 1 commit, 11 days old.
unreferenced_scripts.recommendation: archive_candidate — one-time script with no references

gitignore_coverage.note: .gitignore was NOT in the --changed-files list; no gitignore gap introduced by this diff. All 4 HARNESS_RULES from session-gitignore-propagate.sh are present.

diff_specific_findings.note: In --changed-files mode (Mechanism ii tagging), findings outside the 4 changed files are tagged introduced_in_diff: false and are advisory/non-blocking per AC-2.6(b) default-safe rule.

changed_files_analysis.change: One-line heading rename: '(3) Mtime + literal-task-id glob' -> '(3) Mtime window + machine-readable marker predicate'
Moved dd|mkfs|fdisk|shred destructive-disk block after COMMAND_CONTEXT_STRIPPED setup; changed kill-PID rule to use stripped view; minor comment update
NEW file: e2e pytest tests for Item A rules (dd/mkfs rule + kill-PID rule). Placed in hooks/tests/ following project naming convention (test_<subject>.py).
changed_files_analysis.verdict: clean — new test file correctly placed, correctly named, tests existing hook functionality

codex_consult.feedback_summary: Codex raised 6 issues + 1 observation. Key actionable findings: (1) scripts/todo/*.py entries are false positives from detection script not excluding project-level todo/ directory; (2) product-owner.md orphan finding is a false positive — agent is dynamically referenced as a specialist type; (3) severity for orphaned agents should be major not minor per policy. Codex also suggested filtering findings to only changed files in --changed-files mode, which was rejected as inconsistent with Mechanism (ii) tagging contract.

findings.issue: Scope violation: findings list files outside the 4 changed files. Codex argues --changed-files mode should only emit findings for the diff files.
findings.proposed_fix: Filter findings to only commands/commit.md, hooks/pretool-bash-safety.sh, hooks/lib/bash_context_strip.py, hooks/tests/test_bash_safety_context_rules.py
findings.rationale: This inspector implements Mechanism (ii) — tagging, not filtering. Pre-existing advisory findings are emitted with introduced_in_diff: false and are non-blocking per AC-2.6(b) default-safe rule. Filtering them entirely would hide legitimate full-repo advisory information. The tagging contract allows this; close.md ignores non-introduced findings.

findings.issue: agents/product-owner.md is a false positive — it IS referenced by commands/dev.md, commands/dev-overnight.md, agents/ba.md, agents/pm.md as a specialist agent type.
findings.proposed_fix: Remove the orphaned_subagents entry for product-owner.md
findings.rationale: Verified: product-owner IS referenced in multiple commands and agents as a specialist type. The detection script uses simple text-matching and misses structural/contextual references. Finding retained as advisory with explicit false_positive_likely note.

findings.issue: scripts/todo/*.py entries are false positives. Todo scripts are dynamically invoked by command name via hook-todo-injection.py and correspond to existing commands/skills.
findings.proposed_fix: Remove all scripts/todo/*.py entries from unreferenced_scripts
findings.rationale: Verified: (a) hook-todo-injection.py at line 66-69 dynamically resolves scripts/todo/<cmd-name>.py by command name at runtime; (b) GLOBAL dir scan in detect-orphan-scripts.sh already correctly excludes todo/ with -not -path '*/todo/*'; (c) the project-level scan lacks this exclusion causing false positives for scripts/todo/* that are legitimately referenced dynamically. All scripts/todo/*.py entries removed from findings.

findings.issue: Severity mismatch: orphaned_subagents policy severity is major, not minor.
findings.proposed_fix: Change severity to major if retained
findings.rationale: Per inspection checklist Section 8: orphaned subagents severity = major. Applied: finding retained as advisory with severity corrected to minor only because it is very likely a false positive; if confirmed orphaned in a full-repo scan, severity should be major.

findings.issue: scripts/install/tmp-cleanup-install.sh should require user confirmation before deletion, not be treated as safe-to-delete.
findings.rationale: Correct — the script is an operational install source for a cron cleanup script. safe_to_delete changed to false with requires_user_confirmation note.
Per Mechanism (ii), advisory pre-existing findings are acceptable in --changed-files mode with introduced_in_diff: false. Retained as advisory non-blocking finding.

findings.issue: No new cleanliness issues introduced by the 4 changed files. All introduced_in_diff values are correctly false.
findings.rationale: Confirmed: none of the 4 diff files introduce new orphans, dead code, or misplacement issues.

codex_consult.feedback_incorporated: Applied: (1) product-owner.md finding annotated as likely false positive with verification note; (2) scripts/todo/*.py false positives removed from unreferenced_scripts (all were dynamically referenced via hook-todo-injection.py); (3) scripts/install/tmp-cleanup-install.sh safe_to_delete changed to false with user_confirmation note; (4) severity note added for orphaned_subagents policy. Rejected: scope-filtering finding (Mechanism ii tagging is the correct contract, not filtering).

summary.verdict: CLEAN — no issues introduced by this diff. All findings are pre-existing advisory items tagged introduced_in_diff: false and are non-blocking per AC-2.6(b) default-safe rule.
